title: Database lock contention degrades upstream checkout
summary: A heavy reporting batch job takes a long lock on orders-pg. Query latency on orders-pg jumps ~40x, so checkout requests slow and time out, and api-gateway surfaces 5xx to users. There is no checkout deploy. The fault propagates upward from the database, so blast-radius direction is the key disambiguator.
question: Why did checkout latency and 5xx spike around 14:22 on 2026-06-03 with no deploy?

ground_truth.notes: Checkout is a victim, not the cause: its code is unchanged and its own spans wait on the DB span. Dependency direction (errors originate downstream at the DB and propagate upstream) is what separates cause from symptom.
